name: Netherlands
contextBlurb: As of 2025, the Netherlands applies the EU Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA). Retail users may lawfully buy, hold, and redeem MiCA-compliant stablecoins (EMTs/ARTs). Conduct supervision and CASP (crypto-asset service provider) licensing are handled by the AFM; prudential supervision of token issuers (e.g., reserves, own funds) sits with De Nederlandsche Bank (DNB) under the Netherlands' twin-peaks model. Stablecoins are not legal tender and carry issuer/operational risk.

legislation.title: Apr 2026 ESMA confirmed that the MiCA transitional period ends mid-2026; crypto firms must hold a full MiCA/CASP licence by that deadline. As of April 2026, 199 CASPs are licensed across the EU; no Asset-Referenced Token (ART) issuer has yet been approved.
Nine major European banks, including ING, announced plans to launch a MiCA-compliant euro-denominated stablecoin, supervised by DNB, expected in 2026.
Netherlands-specific transitional period ends (six months); from 2025-07-01 providers must be MiCA-authorised or passported.
Bitvavo announces MiCA authorisation in NL.

keyFigures.name: AFM (Autoriteit Financiële Markten)
keyFigures.role: First point of contact for MiCA CASP licensing and conduct supervision; coordinates with DNB.

keyFigures.name: DNB (De Nederlandsche Bank)
keyFigures.role: Prudential supervisor for MiCA issuers (EMTs/ARTs), capital/own funds and certain prudential topics; continues to superv

news.headline: AFM – Contact & FAQ for CASP licensing, transitional period and AFM/DNB split
DNB – Cryptos and supervision: what you need to know
Digital Watch – ESMA confirms end of MiCA transition period (April 2026)

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: As of 2025, the Netherlands applies the EU Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA). Retail users may lawfully buy, hold, and redeem MiCA-compliant stablecoins (EMTs/ARTs). Conduct supervision and CASP (crypto-asset service provider) licensing are handled by the AFM; prudential supervision of token issuers (e.g., reserves, own funds) sits with De Nederlandsche Bank (DNB) under the Netherlands' twin-peaks model. Stablecoins are not legal tender and carry issuer/operational risk.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: Stablecoins in the Netherlands are legally classified as either e-money tokens (EMTs) or asset-referenced tokens (ARTs) under MiCA, depending on their design. EMTs are treated similarly to electronic money and require full backing in permitted reserve assets.

regulators.name: AFM
regulators.role: First point of contact for MiCA CASP licensing and conduct supervision; coordinates with DNB.

regulators.name: DNB
regulators.role: Prudential supervisor for MiCA issuers (EMTs/ARTs), capital/own funds and certain prudential topics; continues to supervise payment/e-money institutions.
